subject: Project Management
title: When to Use Waterfall
description: A quick, practical guide to When to Use Waterfall.

hook.text: Waterfall works best when three conditions are met: stable requirements, understood technology, and low ambiguity.

buildup.text: Building a bridge, manufacturing a car, or porting an existing system — these have well-known requirements.

discovery.text: When the problem is well-understood and the solution is proven, extensive upfront planning saves time and money.

twist.text: Even in waterfall-friendly contexts, smart teams add feedback loops: design reviews, prototypes, and gate checks.

climax.text: Waterfall isn't dead — it's specialized. For the right project, it's still the most efficient approach.

punchline.text: Know the problem? Know the solution? Waterfall away.

quiz.question: When is waterfall most appropriate?
options: When requirements are stable, technology is understood, and ambiguity is low
When the team has never built anything similar
